title: What happened to Metalink?
description:
I remembered Metalink once again as I was looking for a protocol for downloading files from HTTP mirrors transparently.

It seems like Metalink has succeeded at releasing 2 RFCs (5854 and 6249) but failed to get traction. curl removed its support in 2021 (after ~9 years) and no web browser ever supported it in the first place.

Does anyone know what happened to it and why? Given how popular BitTorrent got, and how big the Web is and how popular CDN services have become, it’s surprising to me that Metalink failed to gain adoption.

(Also happy to hear about similar projects!)
description_plain:
I remembered [Metalink](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metalink) once again as I was looking for a protocol for downloading files from HTTP mirrors transparently.

It seems like Metalink has succeeded at releasing 2 RFCs (5854 and 6249) but failed to get traction. curl [removed](https://daniel.haxx.se/blog/2021/06/07/bye-bye-metalink-in-curl/) its support in 2021 (after ~9 years) and no web browser ever supported it in the first place. 

Does anyone know what happened to it and why? Given how popular BitTorrent got, and how big the Web is and how popular CDN services have become, it’s surprising to me that Metalink failed to gain adoption.

(Also happy to hear about similar projects!)

title: TruffleRuby 34: full Ruby 3.4 compatibility, up to 23% faster parsing, and a new Prism-based Ripper with 20x speedups
I want to show the cli tool I built to the world. How?
description:
I want to show the world what I built. I think it's a really neat, well-thought cli tool that other users would find useful.

But almost every platform (including lobsters) has a policy against posting links to one's own work. I know, I know, you can, if it's just a percentage of your contributions. But I'm not a regular contributor to any of these platforms. Finding cool links and new tools is not my skill.

So, what's the right way to do it? Is there a place where people share the stuff they built (and yes, it will be abused), but cooll-stuff scouters keep an eye on, to find and post using (and helping) their platform karma/points/score?
description_plain:

So, what's the right way to do it? Is there a place where people share the stuff they built (and yes, it will be abused), but cooll-stuff scouters keep an eye on, to find and post using (and helping) their platform karma/points/score?
